pi_items.source: Confirmed via analysis of task.json description, export_result.sh file existence checks, and verifier.py reading logic.
Confirmed via analysis of task.json description, export_result.sh file existence checks, and verifier.py reading logic.
Confirmed via export_result.sh querying places.sqlite for %httpbin.org/headers% and verifying it is a real public endpoint for viewing HTTP headers.
Mozilla/Tor source code (StaticPrefList.yaml) and about:config manual check confirms this is a real preference, and export_result.sh directly parses prefs.js for this exact string.

privileged_info_summary: The target URLs and preference are real, though no pre-existing dataset is used (live OSINT). The agent must navigate to the public API https://httpbin.org/headers to capture headers. The target preference 'privacy.spoof_english' is a real Firefox/Tor preference used in Resist Fingerprinting. Crucially, while the task description instructs setting 'privacy.spoof_english' to the boolean 'false' (and the evaluation script looks for a boolean in prefs.js), Mozilla/Tor source code confirms 'privacy.spoof_english' is actually an integer preference (values 0, 1, 2) by default. In the about:config UI, an agent cannot type 'false' into an integer field; they would have to delete the preference and recreate it as a boolean, or directly modify prefs.js.
